title: FV1Tool Metadata Schema v1
description: JSON Schema for FV-1 SpinASM metadata headers, supporting versioned metadata with required fields for effect documentation, IO configuration, pot labeling, memory allocation, and signal flow graphs.

version.description: Metadata schema version. v1 is default for backward compatibility. v2 adds extended validation and optional fields for future expansion.

effectName.description: Human-readable name of the effect. Used for display and documentation.

io.description: Input/output mode for the effect. Determines audio channel configuration: mono_mono (single in, single out), mono_stereo (single in, stereo out), stereo_stereo (stereo in, stereo out).

pots.description: Array of exactly 3 potentiometer configurations corresponding to POT0, POT1, and POT2 on the FV-1.

id.description: Identifier for the potentiometer. Must be one of pot0, pot1, or pot2.

label.description: Human-readable label for the potentiometer control (e.g., 'Mix', 'Time', 'Feedback'). Displayed in the UI and documentation.

memory.description: Array of delay memory allocations. Each allocation specifies a named delay buffer and its size in samples.

name.description: Identifier for the memory allocation (e.g., 'delay1', 'delayL', 'delayR'). Should match memory references in the SpinASM code.

samples.description: Size of the delay buffer in samples. FV-1 supports up to 32768 samples total across all allocations at 32kHz sample rate.

graph.description: Signal flow graph describing the processing blocks and their connections. Used for visualization only; does not affect code validation or simulation.

nodes.description: Array of node identifiers representing processing blocks in the signal path (e.g., 'input', 'delay', 'filter', 'output').

edges.description: Array of directed edges defining signal flow between nodes. Cycles are allowed for feedback paths.

from.description: Source node identifier. Must exist in the nodes array.

to.description: Destination node identifier. Must exist in the nodes array.
